purpose: Every maintenance / recovery / break-glass operation: what it does, what it excludes, whether it is safe, and what it CANNOT do.
provenance: AST census of CLI verbs (59, argparse add_parser) + Cycle-3 recovery inventory (W9) + Cycle-5 lock census.

cli_surface.policy: *** Do NOT run these speculatively. Tests and read-only verbs only unless the operator asks (project CLAUDE.md). ***

recovery_operations.exclusion: *** store.lock() — THE CORRECT LOCK (SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE) ***

recovery_operations.caller: *** NO PRODUCTION CALLER *** — but ledger_wipe.py:246 ADVERTISES it as THE wipe-rollback path
recovery_operations.exclusion: fcntl.flock on ledger.lock — *** EXCLUDES NOTHING. Mutually invisible to the BEGIN IMMEDIATE every other writer takes. ***
recovery_operations.verdict: *** CRITICAL — RC-4 / C4-F2a. PROVEN BY EXECUTION: a concurrent writer's commit() SUCCEEDS, its data is SILENTLY DISCARDED, AND its deferred media unlinks PROCEED — deleting real .mp4 files whose ledger rows the restore brings back. The ledger comes back claiming the media exists; the .mp4 is gone. ***
recovery_operations.the_damning_detail: the sibling SIX LINES ABOVE (Ledger.snapshot) takes the RIGHT lock. This is not a missing concept — it is one method that did not use the concept its own sibling uses. Three cycles examined this method and none looked six lines up.
recovery_operations.why_the_right_lock_is_still_insufficient: SQLite's write lock is held on the INODE via an open fd. A writer that opened the db BEFORE the os.replace still holds that inode. NO lock acquired beforehand can prevent it. The root fix is to NOT REPLACE THE FILE.
recovery_operations.reachability: an operator following the DOCUMENTED wipe-rollback procedure while the daemon is running

recovery_operations.verdict: SAFE — ADDS orphans ONLY (as SourceState.discovered, INERT until an operator promotes). *** It NEVER retires a source whose file is missing (ledger.py:746-748). *** That is the wipe-safety invariant.
recovery_operations.coupling: COUP-04 — it detects orphans BY FILENAME SHAPE (_SID_RE = ^src_[0-9a-f]{12}$), which hardcodes the format ids.py mints. Changing the id length silently breaks orphan detection with no error.

recovery_operations.verdict: SAFE — the single owner of the recovery transition. REFUSES a healthy source.
T0 RESET — purges disk caches, discards gates, reconcile_moments(sid, {}). Destructive but scoped.
AUTONOMOUS — but *** ONLY for posts with NO submission_id. *** A post with a REAL submission_id is NOT healed by it.
*** THE UNGUARDED FORCE-ANYTHING ESCAPE HATCH. *** --url REQUIRED for terminal states. The SOLE writer of PostState.retired.
accepts ONLY failed/error. *** A `submitting` post is NOT OFFERED. *** All five requeue paths DO clear submission_id.

SAFE-BY-CONSTRUCTION — FULLY inside ONE Ledger.transaction, so a raise halfway rolls back ENTIRELY. Gated: WipeNotConfirmed unless confirmed; SnapshotRequired unless snapshot_is_restorable ACTUALLY OPENS the SQLite file and reads schema_version. *** It writes a .files.txt manifest of media paths but DOES NOT UNLINK MEDIA. ***
recovery_operations.known_gap: MOL-71 — do_wipe_confirm has NO server-side check that do_wipe_preview ran. 'Preview before confirm' is a UI CONVENTION ONLY.

recovery_operations.verdict: rewrites Render.path after a root move. *** Currently a NO-OP in practice: led.renders is always empty. ***
shells launchctl. Boots the KEEPER FIRST on stop so it cannot re-bootstrap the pump.
recovery_operations.critical: the plist BAKES A FULL PATH at install — launchd supplies a bare one.

THE_MISSING_RECOVERY_PATH.statement: *** There is NO recovery — autonomous OR manual — for a post stranded in submitting/needs_reconcile with a REAL submission_id, and NO signal that tells the operator it happened. ***
why: reconcile's escalation and give-up BOTH gate on _is_fake_token, which excludes a real id
a RAISING poll (every Zernio 404) bails out before the ladder is reached
ANY non-empty error_reason permanently suppresses further attention (reconcile.py:767)
the Studio requeue family accepts ONLY failed/error — `submitting` is not offered
the Studio Review revert BLOCKS needs_reconcile/submitting/submitted
THE_MISSING_RECOVERY_PATH.the_only_escape: `fanops resolve <id> failed` then Studio Recover — a two-step CLI break-glass that requires the operator to ALREADY KNOW the post is stranded. Nothing tells them.
THE_MISSING_RECOVERY_PATH.reachability: AN ORDINARY OPERATOR WORKFLOW: a platform rejects a post (IG rejects the aspect ratio) -> PostState.failed with the real sid PRESERVED -> operator sends it back to Review to fix the caption -> re-approves -> the CLAIM commits `submitting` -> the POST is skipped -> stranded forever, with ZERO breadcrumbs (the stale error_reason trips the suppression latch on pass one).

operational_constraints_on_maintenance.OPS-001: *** THE ORCHESTRATION GATE IS STILL ENGAGED — FIVE CONSECUTIVE CYCLES. *** .orchestration/state/ACTIVE = engaged (last touched 2026-07-13). It refuses every subagent spawn outside {fanops-worker, fanops-lander} and refuses any shell command NAMING .orchestration/state/, .cursor/hooks*, or .githooks/ — including a read-only `ls`. Cycles 1, 2, 3, 4 AND 5 were all executed SINGLE-THREADED. Disengage is `orchestrate.py stop` — AN OPERATOR ACTION, not a code change. It remains the single largest constraint on this audit's throughput.
operational_constraints_on_maintenance.never_run_tests_locally: pytest / check-full.sh execute ONLY in GitHub CI on a PR. Parallel wave workers each running the suite crash the machine.
operational_constraints_on_maintenance.never_mass_reformat: no black, no ruff format. The compact one-liner house style is deliberate.
operational_constraints_on_maintenance.one_orchestrator_at_a_time: git fetch + gh pr view the target before merge — parallel orchestrators caused double-merges.
